answer:
**Iqbal's "Bilad-e-Islamiya" (The Abodes of Islam)** is Chapter 2 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 3* (1924), a collection of Urdu poetry by Allama Iqbal.[^1] This poem offers a poetic survey of key Islamic cities—Delhi, Baghdad, Cordoba, Constantinople (Istanbul), and Yathrib (Medina)—celebrating their historical and spiritual significance as "abodes" of Islamic civilization, sovereignty, and the ummah's legacy.[^2][^3][^4][^5][^6]

The English translation opens: "The soil of Delhi is a shrine for the grief-stricken heart; / In every particle, the blood of our forebears lies asleep."[^7] It proceeds to evoke each city's sacred dust, rulers, and cultural radiance, culminating in praise for Yathrib as the Muslim's true homeland: "Ah, Yathrib! You are the Muslim's true homeland, you are his sanctuary."[^8]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 2: "Bilad-e-Islamiya" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/)).

[^2]: Delhi as a "shrine" and "sanctum of the majesty of Islam" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-4ecfd3](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-4ecfd3)).

[^3]: Baghdad as the "garden" of Hijazi culture, where "the Prophet's successors" walked ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-3ddf5a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-3ddf5a)).

[^4]: Cordoba as a "Lamp of Sinai" that lit "the civilization of today" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-43c1b0](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-43c1b0)).

[^5]: Constantinople as the "heart of the Islamic nation," with a voice from Ayyub Ansari's tomb ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-630e3b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-630e3b)).

[^6]: Yathrib (Medina) as the "focal point that gathers all the rays of our soul's devotion" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-f92993](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-f92993)).

[^7]: Chapter opening ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-876603](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-876603)).

[^8]: Chapter closing ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-c3029f](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/02-bilad-e-islamiya/translation/#p-c3029f)).
